content:
3. Tmux
Tmux is a popular open source terminal multiplexer that makes the setup truly enjoyable by allowing persistent terminal sessions.
Installing Tmux on macOS (using Homebrew):
brew install tmuxNormally, SSH sessions disappear the moment your connection drops.
That happens a lot on mobile when:

• switching apps

• locking your phone

• iOS suspending background apps
Without tmux, the terminal session would be terminated right as SSH dropped.
However, with Tmux, you have the ability to create detached session:
tmux new-session -d -s mainThis creates a detached session called “main”, such that every time you want to go back to that session you can do that by executing:
tmux attach -t mainThis reattaches the main session to the current terminal, preserving all progress.
